sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἱκανός

- Transliteration: Hikanos

- Phonetic: hik-an-os'

- Definition:

1. sufficient

a. many enough, enough

b. sufficient in ability, i.e. meet, fit

- Origin: from hiko [hikano or hikneomai, akin to G2240] (to arrive)

- TDNT entry: 07:53,4

- Part(s) of speech: Adjective

- Strong's: From ἵκω hikō (ἱκάνω or ἱκνέομαι akin to G2240; to arrive); competent (as if coming in season) that is ample (in amount) or fit (in character): - able + content enough good great large long (while) many meet much security sore sufficient worthy.
